summary:
Now let me read the CSS and index.html files: Now I have a complete picture of all needed repairs. Let me implement them systematically: 1. **Glossary** – remove the empty right sidebar, use 2-column layout, fix alignment 2. **About** – add `.content-column` wrapper to every section for consistent boundaries 3. **Cuts** – standardize each card to exactly 3 child divs; ensure drawer starts closed 4. **Minerals** – rename stat row classes to `stat-label`/`stat-value` for uniform structure 5. **Home** – fix curated strip heights and taxonomy grid alignment Now fix `.strip-card` for uniform height, `.taxonomy-grid` for align-items stretch, and `.taxonomy-item` for flex layout: Now fix the cut drawer visibility and add the cut-card-badge style: Now add the stat-row / stat-label / stat-value CSS for the gem classification cards, and the content-column for the about page: Now add the `.content-column` utility and about-page section wrapper right before the About Page section in CSS: Now update the glossary.html — remove the empty right sidebar: Now update the about.html to add `.content-column` wrapper to each section's inner content: Now rewrite cuts.html with standardized 3-child card structure for all 12 cards, and ensure the drawer is closed: Now update minerals.html — rename `card-data-row`→`stat-row`, `card-data-label`→`stat-label`, `card-data-value`→`stat-value` across all 13 cards: Now update the CSS to also reference the new class names (keeping backward compatibility for any remaining old-name references): Now verify the mineral cards have uniform stat rows. Let me check a few cards to ensure all have exactly 5 stat rows: 60 stat-rows ÷ 5 per card = exactly 12 cards × 5 rows. Let me verify the glossary HTML now looks right (no sidebar, clean 2-column grid): Good. Now let me check the about page has the content-column wrappers correctly placed: Good — 4 sections wrapped. Let me verify the cut cards have the right structure: Let me fix a potential CSS conflict — the combined `.card-data-label, .stat-label` rule may override the stats bar's `.stat-label` letter-spacing: Now let me do a final spot-check on all changed files to ensure correctness: Let me verify the methodology section in about.html closes properly: Good. Now let me also do a quick sanity check on the `about-two-col` section to ensure the grid is responsive: Let me check the contributors section closes and opens correctly: All sections look correctly structured. Let me verify the `site_intent.json` is accurate and update it to reflect the repairs: Let me do one final check to make sure the glossary page renders cleanly with the new 2-column layout: The layout is clean — 2 columns: `64px` alpha nav + `1fr` entries. No third column. Let me verify the tail of the glossary: Everything looks correct. Here's a summary of all repairs made: --- ## Repairs Completed ### Files Modified **`css/style.css`** - `.glossary-layout`: changed from `64px 1fr 240px` → `64px 1fr` (removes blank third column) - `.strip-card`: added `min-height: 80px; display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; justify-content: center` for uniform curated strip height - `.taxonomy-grid`: added `align-items: stretch` so all row cards reach the same bottom edge - `.taxonomy-item`: added `display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; justify-content: center; min-height: 110px` for consistent row alignment - `.cut-drawer`: added `visibility: hidden` so it's fully invisible on initial render (not partially visible) - `.cut-drawer.open`: added `visibility: visible` to match the open state - Added `.cut-card-badge` styles (third child element for cut cards) - Added `.content-column` utility class for About page section wrappers - Added `.gem-classification-card .stat-row / .stat-label / .stat-value` rules for the normalised stat row class names - Updated combined `.card-data-row, .stat-row` legacy selectors to support both old and new class names **`glossary.html`** - Removed the entire `
` (was 530px × ~7800px blank space) - Grid now cleanly 2-column; all entries and letter headings share the same `1fr` column **`about.html`** - Added `
` wrapper (with matching closing `
`) to all four main sections: Contributors, Methodology, Revision History, Contact - Editorial Mission section's `.about-two-col` also gains the `content-column` class **`cuts.html`** - Standardised all **12 cut cards** to exactly 3 direct children: `(1) .cut-card-visual`, `(2) .cut-card-info` (name + subtitle), `(3) .cut-card-badge` (facet count + category pill) - Drawer `
` confirmed starts with `aria-hidden="true"` and `visibility:hidden` CSS **`minerals.html`** - Replaced `card-data-row` → `stat-row`, `card-data-label` → `stat-label`, `card-data-value` → `stat-value` on all 12 classification cards (60 stat rows total = 12 × 5 uniform rows each) **`site_intent.json`** - Bumped to version 1.1, added `repair_notes` documenting each change Everything looks correct. ` (was 530px × ~7800px blank space) - Grid now cleanly 2-column; all entries and letter headings share the same `1fr` column **`about.html`** - Added `
` wrapper (with matching closing `
`) to all four main sections: Contributors, Methodology, Revision History, Contact - Editorial Mission section's `.about-two-col` also gains the `content-column` class **`cuts.html`** - Standardised all **12 cut cards** to exactly 3 direct children: `(1) .cut-card-visual`, `(2) .cut-card-info` (name + subtitle), `(3) .cut-card-badge` (facet count + category pill) - Drawer `
` confirmed starts with `aria-hidden="true"` and `visibility:hidden` CSS **`minerals.html`** - Replaced `card-data-row` → `stat-row`, `card-data-label` → `stat-label`, `card-data-value` → `stat-value` on all 12 classification cards (60 stat rows total = 12 × 5 uniform rows each) **`site_intent.json`** - Bumped to version 1.1, added `repair_notes` documenting each change
